title: Boundary Folding Produces Sign-Changing Mass Dirac Equation
summary: Discharge the proof obligation that the boundary gluing of the doubled system can be folded into a single Dirac equation on all of space with a mass term m sign(x_1).
assumptions: The gluing condition at the boundary is the one used by Witten in the doubled system.
regime: Folded whole-space reformulation of the boundary problem.
scope: Third proof obligation in the Lecture Three theorem family.

source_anchors.notes: The gluing condition is rewritten as one sign-changing-mass Dirac equation.

representation: Bounded proof obligation for the folding step from boundary gluing to the domain-wall equation.
pass_conditions: The answer states the boundary gluing condition or the two opposite-mass equations.
The answer explains the folding construction.
The answer derives the sign-changing-mass Dirac equation.
mandatory_logical_moves: Fold the two half-space fermions into one whole-space spinor.
Show that the resulting equation has mass term m sign(x_1).
common_failure_patterns: Starting from the sign-changing-mass equation without explaining how it arises from the doubled system.
Treating the wall equation as an unrelated ansatz.
grading_rubric: Full credit requires the boundary-to-domain-wall rewrite, not only the final wall equation.
Partial credit is appropriate if the answer quotes the wall equation but not the folding logic.
retrieval_hints: Use when the answer must show how the boundary problem becomes the Jackiw-Rebbi problem.
